tasks.instruction: Publish a new public poem in English with a short urban night-time free-verse theme, adding three relevant tags and allowing comments.

poem.title: City Night Experiment
poem.body:
Neon rivers hum above the asphalt,
windows blink like patient satellites,
I walk the edge of empty crosswalks,
listening for a story in the lights.

tasks.instruction: Find the current weekly prompt, submit a short poem response to it, then like three prompt responses anywhere on the site that each have fewer than 10 likes.

response_poem.title: Training Prompt Test Poem
response_poem.body:
The clock forgets the names of hours,
the sky misplaces every star,
I write my lines in crooked power,
so you remember who you are.

poems_commented_on.comment_body: Beautiful imagery, especially the forest detail.

poems_commented_on.comment_body: I enjoyed how you described nature in this piece too.

tasks.instruction: Create a new poem, save it as a draft, then schedule it to be published 12 days from now at 9:00 AM.

poem.title: Draft Scheduling Test
poem.body:
This is a test draft poem for scheduling.
It wanders through an unseen room,
Waiting for a future morning,
To quietly and softly bloom.
